retry_reason: Failure indicates either no existing entry (and creation not requested) or an allocation error; retrying without changed conditions is unlikely to succeed.
contract_summary: This is a get-or-create wrapper for neighbour entries. It first attempts to look up an existing neighbour via neigh_lookup, which acquires a reference if found. If not found and creat is true, it creates a new neighbour via neigh_create, which initializes a reference (set to 1). The returned neighbour pointer always carries an elevated reference count that the caller must eventually release with a corresponding put (e.g., neigh_release). On failure (lookup fails and creat is false, or creation fails), NULL is returned and no reference is transferred. The caller must check the return value and handle NULL appropriately.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to obtain a neighbour with an elevated reference count via lookup (get) or creation (set); minimal extra logic.
